title: Parahebe hookeriana
taxonomy: Parahebe hookeriana is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as figworts. The genus Parahebe is part of the subfamily Janianoideae. This species is native to New Zealand and is often found in rocky outcrops and scrublands. Its classification also places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Parahebe hookeriana, also known as the New Zealand spinach or rock daisy, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Parahebe hookeriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Parahebe hookeriana is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into capsule and tablet formulations for its medicinal properties.
